mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced corporate profitability expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive rates above neutral
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) of more than 100,000 jobs month-over-month signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 100,000 jobs in single month
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early-cycle contraction
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances following PAYEMS declines > 100,000, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 60-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with stable or declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates robust consumer demand and tight labor market conditions that support corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both conditions to activate.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong demand and tight labor markets
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances when both RSAFS growth > 0.5% and UNRATE < 4.2%, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while remaining above trend indicate supply-side weakness that constrains economic growth and corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as markets reprrice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances following INDPRO declines > 0.3%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1,200 thousand units annualized while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains below 2.0% annualized, signaling synchronized demand weakness across residential and aggregate economic activity. This interaction triggers negative equity returns over 60 trading days as recession risk rises.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1,200 thousand units AND GDPC1 growth < 2.0% annualized
regime_hint: synchronized demand contraction or pre-recession environment
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when both HOUST < 1,200 and GDPC1 growth < 2.0%, or joint interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.20).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL declines 0.5% or more month-over-month while the federal funds rate remains above 4.5%, indicating that monetary tightening has successfully reduced price pressures. This disinflation signal reduces expectations of further rate hikes, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: disinflation phase within monetary tightening cycle
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances when CPIAUCSL declines > 0.5% and FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises 0.3 percentage points or more in a single month, signaling an abrupt deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers equity market repricing downward as recession probability increases. This always-on trend mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points in single month
regime_hint: labor market shock or early-cycle deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances following UNRATE increases > 0.3pp, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 3.0% annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) grow month-over-month, indicating broad-based demand expansion that supports corporate revenue and earnings growth. This always-on mechanism produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days in expansion regimes.
regime_hint: broad-based economic expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of instances when GDPC1 growth > 3.0% and RSAFS growth is positive, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.20) over any 3-year rolling window.
